quiz_questions.question_text: A histogram shows frequencies using:

options.text: Bars with no gaps between them

quiz_questions.explanation: A histogram uses adjacent bars (no gaps) to display the frequency of data within continuous intervals or bins.

quiz_questions.question_text: A symmetric distribution has:

options.text: No identifiable pattern following conventional understanding
A shape that is roughly a mirror image on both sides of the center
A longer right tail, particularly regarding
A longer left tail, particularly regarding

quiz_questions.explanation: In a symmetric distribution, the left side is approximately a mirror image of the right side, and the mean and median are approximately equal.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a right-skewed distribution, the tail extends to the:
quiz_questions.explanation: A right-skewed (positively skewed) distribution has a longer tail that extends to the right, with most data clustered on the left.

quiz_questions.question_text: A uniform distribution means:

options.text: The data clusters in the center
All intervals have approximately equal frequency

quiz_questions.explanation: In a uniform distribution, data values are spread evenly across all intervals, so the histogram bars are roughly the same height.

quiz_questions.question_text: What shape does a bell curve have?
quiz_questions.explanation: A bell curve (normal distribution) is symmetric and mound-shaped, with data concentrated around the center and tapering off equally on both sides.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a left-skewed distribution, which is typically true?
quiz_questions.explanation: In a left-skewed distribution, the tail extends to the left, pulling the mean lower than the median.

quiz_questions.question_text: A histogram with two peaks is called:
quiz_questions.explanation: A bimodal distribution has two distinct peaks, which may suggest the data comes from two different groups or populations.

quiz_questions.question_text: The interval with the tallest bar on a histogram represents:

options.text: The interval with the lowest frequency
The interval with the highest frequency

quiz_questions.explanation: The height of each bar represents how many data values fall in that interval, so the tallest bar shows the most common interval.

quiz_questions.question_text: In a right-skewed distribution, which relationship is typically true?
quiz_questions.explanation: In a right-skewed distribution, the long right tail pulls the mean to the right, making the mean greater than the median.

quiz_questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a common distribution shape?
quiz_questions.explanation: Symmetric, skewed, uniform, and bimodal are common distribution shapes. 'Diagonal' is not a recognized term for describing data distributions.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a histogram?

options.text: A graph that compares categories using separate bars
A graph that uses bars to display the frequency of data within continuous intervals
A circular graph divided into sectors
A graph that shows individual data points connected by lines

quiz_questions.explanation: A histogram is a bar graph for continuous data where adjacent bars represent frequency counts for consecutive intervals (bins) with no gaps.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'distribution' mean in statistics?

options.text: The range of the data set
The way data values are spread out and arranged across possible values

quiz_questions.explanation: A distribution describes the pattern of how data values are arranged, including their shape, center, and spread.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'skewed' mean when describing a data distribution?

options.text: The distribution is perfectly balanced on both sides
The distribution is not symmetric, with one tail longer than the other

quiz_questions.explanation: A skewed distribution is asymmetric, with data bunched on one side and a longer tail stretching in the other direction.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'normal distribution'?

options.text: A distribution where all values have the same frequency
A symmetric, bell-shaped distribution where data clusters around the mean
A distribution that is always skewed right

quiz_questions.explanation: A normal distribution is the classic bell curve -- symmetric and mound-shaped, with about 68% of data within one standard deviation of the mean.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'frequency'?

options.text: The average of all data values
The median of the data set
The count of how many data values fall in a specific interval or category

quiz_questions.explanation: Frequency is the number of times a data value or group of values occurs. In a histogram, the height of each bar represents the frequency for that interval.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'symmetric' mean in the context of data distributions?

options.text: The data has exactly two modes as observed in standard experiments
The left half of the distribution is approximately a mirror image of the right half
The distribution is flat, particularly regarding mean within the
All values are the same, particularly regarding same as predicted

quiz_questions.explanation: A symmetric distribution has a shape that is roughly the same on both sides of its center. In a symmetric distribution, the mean and median are approximately equal.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is a 'bin' in a histogram?

options.text: An interval or range of values that groups data in a histogram
The space between bars under equilibrium conditions
The height of a bar as typically measured in practice
A single data point following conventional understanding

quiz_questions.explanation: A bin is a consecutive interval on the horizontal axis of a histogram. Each bar represents the count of data values that fall within that bin.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'unimodal' mean?

options.text: A distribution with one clear peak or mode
A flat distribution with no peaks

quiz_questions.explanation: Unimodal means the distribution has a single prominent peak. This is in contrast to bimodal (two peaks) or multimodal (more than two peaks).

quiz_questions.question_text: What is 'positively skewed' another name for?
quiz_questions.explanation: Positively skewed and right-skewed are the same thing. The tail of the distribution extends toward larger (more positive) values on the right.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is 'negatively skewed' another name for?
quiz_questions.explanation: Negatively skewed and left-skewed are the same thing. The tail extends toward smaller (more negative) values on the left.

quiz_questions.question_text: A school nurse records the number of student visits per day for 20 days. Most days have 5-10 visits, but a few days had 25-30 visits during flu season. What shape would this distribution likely have?

options.text: Symmetric, particularly regarding what in accordance
Right-skewed, because the flu-season days create a long right tail
Left-skewed, particularly regarding cluster in most
Uniform, particularly regarding visits as predicted

quiz_questions.explanation: Most values cluster at the low end (5-10), with a few very high values (25-30) stretching the right tail. This creates a right-skewed distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: A city planner collects commute times for 200 residents. The histogram shows a peak around 20-30 minutes with equal tails on both sides. What distribution shape is this?
quiz_questions.explanation: A single peak with approximately equal tails on both sides describes a symmetric, bell-shaped distribution, like a normal curve.

quiz_questions.question_text: A music streaming service tracks the number of songs listened to per user per day. The histogram is nearly flat, with each bar approximately the same height from 0 to 50 songs. What shape is this distribution?
quiz_questions.explanation: When all bars are approximately the same height, the data values are roughly equally distributed across all intervals, forming a uniform distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: A college professor finds that most students score between 85 and 95 on an easy exam, with a few students scoring as low as 40-50. What type of skew does this distribution have?

options.text: Right-skewed, particularly regarding what in the context
Uniform, particularly regarding data when considering all
Left-skewed, because the low scores create a tail stretching to the left

quiz_questions.explanation: Most data clusters at the high end (85-95) with a tail extending to the left toward low scores. This is a left-skewed (negatively skewed) distribution.

quiz_questions.question_text: A fitness center surveys members about hours exercised per week. The histogram has two peaks: one around 2-3 hours and another around 8-10 hours. What shape best describes this distribution?

options.text: Bimodal, suggesting two distinct groups of exercisers

quiz_questions.explanation: Two distinct peaks suggest two groups -- casual exercisers (2-3 hours) and dedicated exercisers (8-10 hours). This bimodal pattern often reveals subgroups within the data.

quiz_questions.question_text: A real estate agent plots home prices in a neighborhood. Most homes are $200,000-$400,000, but a few luxury homes are $1,000,000+. Which is likely true about the mean and median?

options.text: Mean > Median, because the expensive homes pull the mean to the right
Cannot be determined, particularly regarding likely in
Mean < Median, particularly regarding which under the
Mean = Median, particularly regarding real when

quiz_questions.explanation: The luxury homes create a right-skewed distribution. In right-skewed data, the high values pull the mean above the median.

quiz_questions.question_text: A teacher creates a histogram of student heights in a class. The histogram is approximately bell-shaped. About what percentage of students fall within one standard deviation of the mean?
quiz_questions.explanation: For a normal (bell-shaped) distribution, approximately 68% of data falls within one standard deviation of the mean, according to the empirical rule.

quiz_questions.question_text: A factory measures widget diameters. When they plot a histogram, they see a single peak centered at the target diameter with equal tails. Should the factory manager be concerned about the production process?

options.text: Cannot be determined from a histogram
Yes, because the distribution should be uniform
No, because a symmetric distribution centered on the target suggests the process is well-calibrated
Yes, because the data is spread out

quiz_questions.explanation: A symmetric distribution centered on the target diameter indicates the manufacturing process is producing widgets that consistently cluster around the desired size.

quiz_questions.question_text: An online retailer tracks delivery times. The histogram shows most deliveries arrive in 2-4 days, with some taking up to 15 days. For advertising purposes, should they report the mean or median delivery time?

options.text: Mode, because it shows the most common time
Mean, because it includes all delivery times
Median, because the right-skewed data makes the mean higher than the typical delivery time
Range, because customers want to know the extremes

quiz_questions.explanation: The distribution is right-skewed (most deliveries are fast, with a few very slow ones). The median better represents the typical delivery experience because it is not pulled up by outliers.

quiz_questions.question_text: A survey of household incomes in a small town produces a right-skewed histogram. A journalist reports the median income as $45,000. What can you infer about the mean income?

options.text: The mean is likely greater than $45,000, pulled up by high earners
The mean cannot be compared to the median

quiz_questions.explanation: In a right-skewed distribution, high values in the right tail pull the mean above the median. Therefore the mean income is likely greater than the $45,000 median.
